subject: [DCAEGEN2] Add support for configMaps
commit_message:
[DCAEGEN2] Add support for configMaps

Enhance the DCAE microservice deployment template to
generate a volume mount and a volume for configMaps
whose names and mount paths are specified in the
microservice's values.yaml.

Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2690
Signed-off-by: Jack Lucas <jflos@sonoris.net>
Change-Id: Id2fbce07ab8199f4566b34a99df540f16fd937b0

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Ok I believe we need to discuss this during oom meeting together with Sylvain.
For me there is no point in providing additional abstraction layer for every part of deployment template. I'd rather allow to put a piece of yaml in values for that

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> Ok I believe we need to discuss this during oom meeting together with Sylvain.
> For me there is no point in providing additional abstraction layer for every part of deployment template. I'd rather allow to put a piece of yaml in values for that

-1 on few inline comments noted.

Hey Krzysztof - The usecase here is primarily for certain DCAE Microservice requiring Configmap to be mounted into application; we have this capability supported under Cloudify/Blueprint deployment (where same parameters - configmapname, mountpath) are provided as input and K8s plugin setup the volumemount). As all DCAE services will be using dcaegen2-services-common.microserviceDeployment template, hence need this change to support in generic manner under helm migration.

Btw, not sure what you refer by including yaml directly in values.yaml? Would you have an example?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> (2 comments)
> 
> > Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> > 
> > Ok I believe we need to discuss this during oom meeting together with Sylvain.
> > For me there is no point in providing additional abstraction layer for every part of deployment template. I'd rather allow to put a piece of yaml in values for that
> 
> -1 on few inline comments noted.
> 
> Hey Krzysztof - The usecase here is primarily for certain DCAE Microservice requiring Configmap to be mounted into application; we have this capability supported under Cloudify/Blueprint deployment (where same parameters - configmapname, mountpath) are provided as input and K8s plugin setup the volumemount). As all DCAE services will be using dcaegen2-services-common.microserviceDeployment template, hence need this change to support in generic manner under helm migration.
> 
> Btw, not sure what you refer by including yaml directly in values.yaml? Would you have an example?

Yeah sure. Just to be 100% clear I'm fine with the functionality that you are trying to achieve here there is no issues with that. I've just concerns about the way of achieving it

We had a similar discussion around secrets. We had two approaches:
1) Define an "abstraction layer" for microservices to add environment variables
2) Allow microservices to inject a pieces of yaml into their values.yaml that would be then directly copied to the right place in the deployment.yaml

With envs we decided to go with #1 as it was your team preference but as this gets popping up here and there I'd like to have this discussion again

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> > 
> > (2 comments)
> > 
> > > Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> > > 
> > > Ok I believe we need to discuss this during oom meeting together with Sylvain.
> > > For me there is no point in providing additional abstraction layer for every part of deployment template. I'd rather allow to put a piece of yaml in values for that
> > 
> > -1 on few inline comments noted.
> > 
> > Hey Krzysztof - The usecase here is primarily for certain DCAE Microservice requiring Configmap to be mounted into application; we have this capability supported under Cloudify/Blueprint deployment (where same parameters - configmapname, mountpath) are provided as input and K8s plugin setup the volumemount). As all DCAE services will be using dcaegen2-services-common.microserviceDeployment template, hence need this change to support in generic manner under helm migration.
> > 
> > Btw, not sure what you refer by including yaml directly in values.yaml? Would you have an example?
> 
> Yeah sure. Just to be 100% clear I'm fine with the functionality that you are trying to achieve here there is no issues with that. I've just concerns about the way of achieving it
> 
> We had a similar discussion around secrets. We had two approaches:
> 1) Define an "abstraction layer" for microservices to add environment variables
> 2) Allow microservices to inject a pieces of yaml into their values.yaml that would be then directly copied to the right place in the deployment.yaml
> 
> With envs we decided to go with #1 as it was your team preference but as this gets popping up here and there I'd like to have this discussion again

Sure, we can discuss tomorrow. Also fyi, in order to faciliate design-time integration (MOD) and dynamic onboarding - there is work underway to build Helm chart generator tool (DCAEGEN2-2694) for Istanbul which will use the base dcae-service templates. With abstraction (#1), chart generation usecase are straightforward and follows defined set of attributes/properties. Individual yaml injections (#2) though provides flexibility, are useful when charts are manually created. For automated flows, having the scope predefined and abstracted input works better.
